c2a5ea5f-0a26-4efc-9a42-72b0961eae0c.content:
MY HEALTH SCHOOL
AI Chatbot Knowledge Base Document
Type 2 Diabetes Reversal Program
Founded by Dr. Prabhakar Raj  |  www.myhealthschool.in
1. About My Health School
1.1 Company Overview
My Health School is India's #1 natural Type 2 Diabetes Reversal Program. Founded by Dr. Prabhakar Raj, the program has successfully transformed the lives of more than 15,000 people, helping them achieve a diabetic-free and medicine-free life through holistic, natural methods.
1.2 Our Mission
To reverse Type 2 diabetes naturally, eliminate dependency on medicines, and empower individuals to lead a healthy, fulfilling, medicine-free life through lifestyle transformation, diet, and natural therapy.
1.3 Why Choose My Health School?
Proven program with 15,000+ success stories
Reverse blood sugar levels without harmful side effects
Come off medicines within 6 months under expert guidance
Holistic approach: diet + lifestyle + natural therapy
4.8-star rating from real customer reviews
Featured in leading national media publications
Personalized coaching by Dr. Prabhakar Raj and his expert team
2. Program Details
2.1 The 6-Month Diabetes Reversal Program
The flagship program is designed to reverse Type 2 diabetes naturally within 6 months. The program combines a science-backed diet plan, lifestyle changes, and natural therapies to restore healthy blood sugar levels without relying on medications.
2.2 What the Program Includes
Personalized diet and nutrition plan tailored for diabetics
Lifestyle modification guidance (sleep, stress, exercise)
Natural therapy protocols
Regular monitoring and follow-up with health coaches
Access to a supportive community of 15,000+ members
Weekly expert sessions and Q&A with Dr. Prabhakar Raj
Corporate wellness programs for organizations
2.3 Who Can Join?
Individuals diagnosed with Type 2 Diabetes
Pre-diabetics looking to prevent full onset
People looking to reduce or eliminate diabetes medications
Corporate employees with high-stress, sedentary lifestyles
Anyone seeking a natural, holistic health transformation
3. Frequently Asked Questions – About My Health School
Q: What is My Health School?
A: My Health School is India's leading natural Type 2 Diabetes Reversal Program founded by Dr. Prabhakar Raj. It has helped 15,000+ people reverse diabetes and become medicine-free within 6 months.
Q: Who founded My Health School?
A: My Health School was founded by Dr. Prabhakar Raj, a renowned health expert dedicated to natural diabetes reversal. He combines traditional wisdom with modern health science to help patients heal naturally.
Q: How many people has My Health School helped?
A: More than 15,000 people have successfully transformed their health through My Health School's program and are living a diabetic-free, medicine-free life.
Q: How can I contact My Health School?
A: You can contact us through our website at www.myhealthschool.in. Click on the 'Contact Us' link to submit your inquiry or book a consultation.
Q: Is this program available online?
A: Yes, My Health School offers online support and coaching so you can access the program from anywhere in India or abroad.
Q: What is the rating of My Health School?
A: My Health School holds a 4.8-star rating based on Google Reviews from thousands of satisfied customers.
4. Diabetes – Core Knowledge Base
4.1 What Is Diabetes?
Diabetes is a chronic metabolic condition in which the body is unable to properly regulate blood glucose (sugar) levels. It occurs either because the pancreas does not produce enough insulin (Type 1) or because the body does not effectively use the insulin it produces (Type 2).
4.2 Types of Diabetes
Type 1 Diabetes
An autoimmune condition where the immune system attacks insulin-producing beta cells in the pancreas. Requires lifelong insulin therapy. Typically diagnosed in childhood or adolescence.
Type 2 Diabetes
The most common type (90–95% of all cases). The body becomes resistant to insulin or doesn't produce enough. Strongly linked to lifestyle factors such as diet, physical inactivity, and obesity. This is the type that My Health School's program targets for natural reversal.
Gestational Diabetes
Occurs during pregnancy. Blood sugar levels become elevated due to hormonal changes. Usually resolves after delivery but increases the risk of Type 2 diabetes later in life.
Pre-Diabetes
A condition where blood sugar levels are higher than normal but not yet high enough to be classified as Type 2 diabetes. A critical window to reverse the condition through lifestyle changes.
4.3 Normal vs. Diabetic Blood Sugar Levels
5. Frequently Asked Questions – Diabetes
Q: What causes Type 2 diabetes?
A: Type 2 diabetes is caused by a combination of insulin resistance (the body's cells don't respond properly to insulin) and insufficient insulin production. Key risk factors include an unhealthy diet high in refined carbohydrates and sugar, physical inactivity, obesity (especially belly fat), chronic stress, poor sleep, family history, and age above 45.
Q: What are the symptoms of diabetes?
A: Common symptoms include frequent urination, excessive thirst, unexplained weight loss, extreme fatigue, blurred vision, slow-healing wounds or sores, frequent infections, tingling or numbness in hands and feet, and dark skin patches (acanthosis nigricans). Some people have no symptoms at all, especially in the early stages.
Q: Can Type 2 diabetes be reversed naturally?
A: Yes! Type 2 diabetes can be reversed naturally through significant lifestyle changes, dietary modifications, weight management, regular physical activity, stress reduction, and proper sleep. My Health School's 6-month program has helped 15,000+ people achieve reversal and become medicine-free using exactly these methods.
Q: How long does it take to reverse diabetes?
A: With the right guidance and commitment, many participants in My Health School's program see significant improvement in blood sugar levels within weeks. Full reversal – coming off medicines – typically happens within 3 to 6 months, depending on current sugar levels and consistency with the program.
Q: What foods should a diabetic avoid?
A: Diabetics should avoid refined sugars and sweets (candy, cakes, pastries), white bread, white rice, white pasta, sugary beverages (sodas, juices, energy drinks), fried and processed foods, trans fats, and excessive alcohol. These spike blood glucose rapidly and worsen insulin resistance.
Q: What foods are good for diabetics?
A: Beneficial foods include non-starchy vegetables (spinach, broccoli, cauliflower, bitter gourd), whole grains (millets, oats, brown rice in moderation), legumes (lentils, chickpeas), nuts and seeds (almonds, walnuts, flaxseeds), berries, lean proteins (fish, chicken, eggs, paneer), and healthy fats (olive oil, coconut oil, avocado).
Q: What is HbA1c and why is it important?
A: HbA1c (Glycated Hemoglobin) is a blood test that reflects your average blood sugar level over the past 2–3 months. It is the gold standard for diabetes monitoring. An HbA1c below 5.7% is normal, 5.7–6.4% indicates pre-diabetes, and 6.5% or above confirms diabetes. Bringing HbA1c below 6% is a key goal in the reversal journey.
Q: Is exercise important for diabetes?
A: Absolutely. Exercise is one of the most powerful natural tools to reverse diabetes. Physical activity improves insulin sensitivity, helps muscles absorb glucose without insulin, reduces belly fat, and lowers blood sugar levels directly. Walking 30–45 minutes daily, yoga, and resistance training are highly recommended for diabetics.
Q: What role does stress play in diabetes?
A: Chronic stress raises cortisol levels, which in turn raises blood sugar and promotes insulin resistance. Managing stress through yoga, meditation, deep breathing, adequate sleep, and work-life balance is a critical pillar of diabetes reversal at My Health School.
Q: Can diabetes cause other health problems?
A: Yes. Poorly managed diabetes can lead to serious complications including heart disease, stroke, kidney disease (nephropathy), nerve damage (neuropathy), eye problems (retinopathy), foot problems, skin infections, and erectile dysfunction. This is why reversal – not just management – is so important.
Q: What is insulin resistance?
A: Insulin resistance occurs when the body's cells stop responding effectively to insulin. As a result, the pancreas produces more and more insulin to compensate, eventually exhausting itself. Insulin resistance is the root cause of Type 2 diabetes and can be reversed with the right diet and lifestyle changes.
Q: Can thin people get Type 2 diabetes?
A: Yes. While obesity is a major risk factor, thin people can also develop Type 2 diabetes, especially if they have high belly fat (visceral fat), a sedentary lifestyle, poor diet, family history, or chronic stress. This is called TOFI – Thin Outside, Fat Inside.
Q: What is the best diet for reversing diabetes?
A: A low-glycemic, whole-foods diet is most effective. This includes non-starchy vegetables, millets, legumes, healthy fats, and adequate protein. Avoiding sugar, refined carbohydrates, and processed foods is essential. My Health School provides personalized diet plans customized for each participant.
Q: Do I need to take insulin if I have Type 2 diabetes?
A: Not necessarily. Most Type 2 diabetics can reverse the condition without insulin through lifestyle changes. Insulin is sometimes prescribed when sugar levels are dangerously high, but the goal of programs like My Health School is to reduce and eliminate medication dependency through natural reversal.
Q: How is Type 2 diabetes diagnosed?
A: Diagnosis is made through blood tests: fasting blood sugar of 126 mg/dL or above, a random blood sugar of 200 mg/dL or above with symptoms, a 2-hour oral glucose tolerance test of 200 mg/dL or above, or an HbA1c of 6.5% or above. A doctor confirms the diagnosis with repeat testing.
Q: What is the glycemic index (GI)?
A: The Glycemic Index (GI) measures how quickly a food raises blood sugar levels. Low-GI foods (below 55) are preferred for diabetics as they cause a slow, gradual rise in blood sugar. Examples include oats, lentils, most vegetables, and berries. High-GI foods like white bread, white rice, and sugary drinks should be avoided.
6. Lifestyle Tips for Diabetes Reversal
6.1 The 5 Pillars of Reversal
Diet & Nutrition – Eat low-glycemic, anti-inflammatory, whole foods
Physical Activity – Walk daily, practice yoga, add resistance training
Stress Management – Meditate, breathe deeply, maintain work-life balance
Quality Sleep – Aim for 7–8 hours; poor sleep raises blood sugar
Natural Therapy – Herbal support, sun exposure, intermittent fasting under guidance
6.2 Daily Habits That Lower Blood Sugar
Drink 8–10 glasses of water daily
Walk 30 minutes after meals
Eat smaller, more frequent meals instead of large portions
Include bitter gourd (karela) juice in your morning routine
Practice 10 minutes of deep breathing or pranayama daily
Go to bed by 10:30 PM and wake up early
Avoid eating 2–3 hours before bedtime
7. How to Get Started with My Health School
If you or a loved one is struggling with Type 2 Diabetes and wants to reverse it naturally, My Health School is here to help. Here's how to begin your journey:
"Reverse Your Sugar and Come Out of Medicine in 6 Months."
– Dr. Prabhakar Raj, Founder, My Health School
